_comment: persona-mvp-kit project settings. Hooks deterministically enforce the kit's bright lines. Permissions are conservative. autoMode.hard_deny absolutely blocks destructive commands. statusLine shows kit state at all times.

permissions._comment_personas_in_ask: personas.md and mvp-spec.md must be EDITABLE (the kit's workflow evolves them) but every edit should prompt the user for confirmation. They are NOT denied; they are explicitly in `ask` to make the prompt unavoidable.

_comment_autoMode: Auto mode config (v2.1.114+). `$defaults` token composes with the built-in classifier rules instead of replacing them (week 17 feature). hard_deny is unconditional, even with allow exceptions (v2.1.128+).
_comment_statusLine: Bottom-of-screen status bar showing kit state: personas/spec/runs/gap-log/branch.
_comment_subagentStatusLine: When a subagent runs, show which one. Substitutes {subagent_name} at render time.
_comment_attribution: Auto-include kit + persona attribution in every commit and PR Claude creates. Customize via $PERSONA env var or edit at run time.
_comment_includeGitInstructions: Tell Claude to follow git conventions from CLAUDE.md (it should know without this; reinforces).
_comment_spinnerTipsOverride: Kit-specific reminders shown during spinners. Reinforces the bright lines while Claude is thinking.

tips: Did you verify personas.md is user-confirmed?
Is the mvp-spec.md success criterion concrete + measurable?
Did you run the workflow as the persona before claiming done?
Is the fix at root cause, or is it a symptom bandage?
Will the persona use this tomorrow morning over their current workflow?

_comment_autoMemory: Auto-memory location. Default per-project; kit doesn't override. Mention in INSTALL.md.
_comment_disableSkillShellExecution: The kit's skills use `!shell` dynamic context injection. If you disable it, persona-driven-mvp / brutal-honest-review / run-the-workflow can't read live project state. Don't set this to true unless you're hardening for a hostile environment.
_comment_hooks: Five hook events register the kit's enforcement layer.
